title: Puerto Banus Boat Rental for a Family Day: Costs and Tips 2026
html:
Set out from Puerto Banus marina on a family boat trip that combines glamour with the raw beauty of the Costa del Sol. A half-day charter for four starts at around 390€, and you can easily compare verified listings with BoatHire24 to find the perfect vessel for your group.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Puerto Banus itself is the main departure hub, with its landmark marina lined with designer boutiques and restaurants. Just west, the ancient fishing port of Puerto José Banús offers a quieter launch point. For a quick getaway, head south-east to Marbella harbour, where you can pick up provisions. Further along the coast, Cabopino marina provides a sandy beach and a nature reserve backdrop. The Bay of Marbella stretches from Puerto Banus to the Cabo Pino lighthouse, offering calm waters for beginners. Nearby Estepona, with its charming old town, is a popular stop for a lunchtime mooring. One option worth a look is the Maiora 26m for hire.

Which boat to rent for a family day

  1. Rib or speedboat – best for a fun, fast trip to a secluded cove; accommodates 6–8 guests; from 450€ for a half-day.
  2. Motor yacht (e.g. Sunseeker or Azimut) – ideal for a comfortable family day with cabin space; sleeps 8–10; from 1,200€ for eight hours.
  3. Sailing catamaran (Lagoon or similar) – stable and spacious, perfect for groups with children; carries 12–14; from 800€ for a half-day.
  4. Luxury flybridge (such as the Maiora 26m for hire) – offers panoramic views and a sun deck; maximum 12 guests; from 2,500€ for a full day.
  5. Small motorboat without licence – easy to drive and budget-friendly; fits 4–6; from 290€ for four hours.

You can browse these options and book directly through platforms like BoatHire24, which provide detailed specifications and transparent pricing.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

Half-day charter prices range from 390€ for a small motorboat to over 3,000€ for a crewed superyacht. Here is a typical cost breakdown:

  • Shared tour / group excursion: from 80€ per person for a two-hour trip.
  • Small motorboat half-day (without crew): 290€–500€.
  • Catamaran full-day (with crew for up to 12): 1,200€–2,000€.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (12 guests): 2,500€–6,000€.

Booking a family day out means you can also save by choosing a vessel from a local owner. Many operators allow you to rent a boat in Puerto Banus for just a few hours, keeping costs manageable.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

Most small motorboats under 15 horsepower do not require a licence in Spanish waters, but larger vessels require a valid Patrón de Navegación Básica or equivalent. If you hire a vessel with a skipper, no licence is needed on your part. The operator will confirm the exact requirements for each boat. For a stress-free family day, chartering a crewed boat is the simplest option, leaving you free to enjoy the scenery.

Local tip: the afternoon sea breeze, known locally as the Levante, picks up around 2pm in summer. Plan your route to return before the wind strengthens if you are driving a small boat yourself.

Season and weather for a Puerto Banus family trip

The high season for boating runs from June to September, when temperatures hover around 30°C and the sea is warm. July and August are the busiest months, so book early to secure your preferred vessel. The prevailing wind is the Poniente, a light westerly that creates calm conditions in the morning. Early departures are recommended to enjoy flat water and avoid afternoon chop.

At a glance

  • Price range: 290€–6,000€ per trip
  • Peak season: June to September
  • Typical trip duration: 4–8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 4–12 persons
  • Currency: EUR
  • Nearest major airport: Málaga-Costa del Sol (50 km)

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I bring food and drink on board?

Yes, most charter boats allow you to bring your own picnic and beverages, but some crewed yachts include catering and bar packages.

Is it safe to take children on a boat trip?

All vessels are equipped with life jackets for every passenger, and skippers are experienced in family-friendly routes along the coast.

What happens if the weather is bad on the day?

Most operators offer a full refund or rescheduling if the harbour authority issues a weather warning, so you are not out of pocket.

Can I anchor and swim during the trip?

Yes, it is common to stop at a cove such as Cabo Pino or near the Marbella shoreline for swimming and snorkelling.

How booking works

Using a marketplace like BoatHire24, you can filter by boat type, capacity, and price, then send a free enquiry to the owner. The platform handles payments securely and provides a straightforward cancellation policy. For a full overview of the process, read how boat rental works on the website.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own. In 2026, families continue to discover the joy of exploring the Costa del Sol from the water. If you own a vessel and would like to offer it for charter, you can check availability and become a host on the platform.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
